description: An authority-governed code value. The @context URI identifies the code system authority (e.g. UN ISIC, UNESCO ISCED, India NSQF). The @type identifies the class of code within that system. The code is the actual value. This pattern avoids hardcoding country-specific enumerations into the schema.

@context.description: URI of the canonical code system authority. Examples: "https://unstats.un.org/unsd/classifications/Econ/isic" (ISIC), "https://uis.unesco.org/en/topic/international-standard-classification-education-isced" (ISCED), "https://www.nqfindia.org/" (India NSQF).

@type.description: Code class within the identified context. Examples: "IndustryCode", "FieldOfEducationCode", "QualificationLevel".

code.description: The actual code value as defined by the authority. Examples: "8299" (ISIC: other business support), "0613" (ISCED: software), "4" (NSQF Level 4).

label.description: Human-readable label for the code (optional, for display).

title: CodedValue
